_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL - PTERODACTYL.IO
name: Assetto Corsa Evo (Proton)
author: micdaffy5@gmail.com
description:
Custom Pterodactyl Egg for the Assetto Corsa Evo dedicated server running natively via Proton.

*** REQUIRED PREPARATION ***
To bypass Pterodactyl read-only restrictions and allow the dashboard to write to configuration files, you MUST build a local Docker wrapper before installing this server. Run these commands on your host via SSH:

mkdir -p ~/acevo-build && cd ~/acevo-build
cat <<EOF > Dockerfile
FROM zino1337/acevo-server:latest
USER root
RUN rm -rf /data && ln -s /home/container /data
RUN rm -rf /root && ln -s /home/container /root
USER 1000
EOF
docker build -t acevo-ptero:latest .

*** PORT & DASHBOARD SETUP ***
1. Network Allocations: Only assign the Game TCP and Game UDP ports in the Pterodactyl Network tab (typically the same port). Do NOT allocate port 8090.
2. Dashboard Access: The web dashboard runs on internal port 8090. To access it, route traffic via a local reverse proxy (like NPMplus) to the container's internal IP on port 8090.
3. Game Configuration: Configure the server name, cars, track, and rules exclusively through the Web Dashboard. Do NOT add Pterodactyl variables for game settings (e.g., SERVER_NAME, ADMIN_PASSWORD), as the startup script will overwrite your dashboard saves.

*** SECURITY & PERMISSIONS ***
* Docker Permissions: The build process elevates to `root` only to create necessary system symlinks for persistent storage. It explicitly drops privileges to USER 1000 before the image is finalized and executed, ensuring the server runs in an unprivileged user-space.
* Steam Credentials: Pterodactyl environment variables are stored in plaintext. It is highly recommended to use a dedicated "burner" Steam account for the Steam Username/Password variables rather than your personal account.

installation.script:
#!/bin/ash
echo "Initialization complete. Game files will be handled by the Docker container upon first startup."

variables.name: Server TCP Port
variables.description: The TCP port used for the server's web interface and API polling. Must be unique for every running server.
